focus: Should .opencode/plugins/README.md remain only an entrypoint inventory, or should it be expanded because the root README points to it as the plugin contract?
noveltyJustification: Converted the prior plugin README gap into a contract-routing answer: root README explicitly delegates the goal plugin contract to .opencode/plugins/README.md, while that README remains entrypoint-first, says 'Both plugins' despite five entrypoints, and omits mk-goal cleanup env vars, store_health and mutation details now present in source.

graphEvents.label: Should .opencode/plugins/README.md remain only an entrypoint inventory or expand because root README points to it as plugin contract?
README.md lines 1230-1233 document /goal and point to .opencode/plugins/README.md for the plugin contract
.opencode/plugins/README.md lines 42-50 list mk-goal.js as one current entrypoint with a short role description
.opencode/plugins/README.md lines 68-115 say Both plugins and document advisor/code-graph config, not mk-goal config
mk-goal.js lines 30-42, 825-899, 1602-1675 and 1758-1829 define cleanup env vars, archive/sweep behavior, store_health and mutation output
Glob found .opencode/commands/goal_opencode.md and no .opencode/commands/goal.md; live file heading remains /goal
Plugin README should expand with compact mk-goal contract details if root README keeps pointing to it as the plugin contract
Live command filename remains goal_opencode.md; /goal is the user-facing command label, so docs should distinguish invocation from physical filename

label: .opencode/plugins/README.md should not remain only an entrypoint inventory while root README points to it as the goal plugin contract
Live command filename remains .opencode/commands/goal_opencode.md; /goal is the user-facing command label and docs should distinguish invocation from physical filename
system-skill-advisor README still says live OpenCode-run tool invocation is under investigation
Plugin README configuration section says Both plugins even though the current entrypoint table lists five plugin files, including mk-goal.js

direction: Treat .opencode/plugins/README.md as inventory-only without changing root README
reason: Root README currently points to .opencode/plugins/README.md as the plugin contract, so inventory-only content leaves users without the delegated mk-goal contract details.
